chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ بَعْدَ اغْتِسَالِهِ مِنَ الطِّيبِ وَ الصَّيْدِ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 79 – What is allowed for the one in Ihraam after his washing, from the perfume, and the hunting, and other than that, before he exclaims Talbiyya

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/254)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/254)
text: لَا تَدَّهِنْ حِينَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ بِدُهْنٍ فِيهِ مِسْكٌ وَ لَا عَنْبَرٌ مِنْ أَجْلِ رَائِحَةٍ تَبْقَى فِي رَأْسِكَ بَعْدَ مَا تُحْرِمُ وَ ادَّهِنْ بِمَا شِئْتَ مِنَ الدُّهْنِ حِينَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ فَإِذَا أَحْرَمْتَ فَقَدْ حَرُمَ عَلَيْكَ الدُّهْنُ حَتَّى تَحِلَّ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘You should not massage with oil when you intend to wear Ihraam, with oil in which is Musk or Amber, due to the reason of its aroma remaining in your head after having worn Ihraam; and you can massage with whatever you so desire to from the oils, when you intend to wear Ihraam. So when you wear Ihraam, then the oil becomes Prohibited unto you until you are free (from Ihraam)’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘You must not apply any oil which has musk or ambergris; for its perfume remains in your head after Ihram. You can apply oil when you want to assume the state of Ihram, once you are in the state of Ihram; applying becomes unlawful until you come out of the state of Ihram.’”
